examples.title: Dynamic Stack Structure and Initialization
examples.description: Complete C structure definition and initialization function for a dynamic stack
examples.explanation: Defines the stack structure with dynamic array pointer, capacity tracking, and top index. Initialization allocates minimum capacity array. isEmpty checks if top==-1. destroyStack properly frees all allocated memory to prevent leaks.
examples.timeComplexity: initStack: O(1), isEmpty: O(1), destroyStack: O(1)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements, plus O(1) overhead for structure

examples.title: Push and Resize Operations with Doubling
examples.description: Push function with automatic resizing when stack is full
examples.explanation: push() checks if stack is full (top >= capacity-1), doubles capacity if needed using resize(), then increments top and stores element. peek() safely retrieves top element. Time complexity: O(1) amortized for push, O(1) for peek.
examples.timeComplexity: push: O(1) amortized, O(n) worst case when resizing; peek: O(1)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space for push; stack itself uses O(n)

examples.title: Pop with Shrink-to-Fit Policy
examples.description: Pop function with automatic shrinking when utilization drops below 25%
examples.explanation: pop() retrieves top element, decrements top, then checks if shrinking is needed. Shrink threshold is 1/4 of capacity (not 1/2) to prevent thrashing. Maintains minimum capacity to avoid excessive shrinking. Time complexity: O(1) amortized.
examples.timeComplexity: pop: O(1) amortized, O(n) worst case when shrinking; getSize: O(1); getCapacity: O(1)
